title: IHF AEGING GFAP + Iba1 + NeuN
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7ivlw","text":"Immunofluorescence for the detection of GFAP, Iba1, and NeuN in rat brain sections, with the aim of characterising the organisation and status of glial and neuronal cells in fixed rat brains, from which 30 µm thick coronal sections were obtained using cryostat sectioning.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"1eon","text":"This protocol is optimised for cryosections of 30 µm-thick adult rat brains that have been fixed in PFA and cryoprotected in 30% sucrose prior to embedding in OCT.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"a6ffu","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c50ve","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"djggc","text":"To succeed in this protocol, you need to have prior knowledge of the techniques involved in free flotation and immunohistochemistry, as well as knowing how to proceed. If you do not yet have the necessary experience, seek expert advice to learn the technical details.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"2mrbf","text":"Good luck!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"3s4q","text":"DAPI is potentially mutagenic","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dj3q2","text":"Use laboratory coat and globes. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"c1r4a","text":"It is important that the tissue is not folded during the process, since this improves the antigen-antibody bond.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Select the slices to be performed and place one slice and citrate into 2 ml tubes. Place one slice in each tube and ensure they are completely submerged.
Add 500 µl/well of blocking Buffer and incubate at RT
Remove primary antibody
Remove Secondary antibody
Bring the VECTASHIELD® HardSet™ to room temperature.
Add 500 µl/well of the primary antibody to each well and incubate at 4 °C with gentle shaking overnight
Prepare Primary antibody dilution GFAP 1/1000 , NeuN 1/1000 and Iba1 1/5000 in antibody diluent
Remove the blocking buffer
Place around 3 slices/well on multi-well plate and wash with 500 ul wash buffer (Wb) 3 times x 5 min each. Kindly ensure that all the tissue in the well is thoroughly covered.
Place the tubes in the thermoblock at 80ºC for 20 minutes. Shaking gently is not compulsory.
Add 500 µl/well of the Secondary antibody to each well and incubate at RT with gentle shaking
Prepare Secondary antibody dilution Anti-Chicken 488 1/500 for GFAP Anti- Mouse 555 1/500 for NeuN Anti-Rabbit 647 1/500 for Iba1
Add 500 µl/well Wb and shaking incubation for 5 min and remove buffer (Repeat this step minimum 3 times).
Add 500 µl/well Wb and shaking incubation for 5 min and remove buffer (Repeat this step minimum 3 times)
Add 500 µl/well of the DAPI to each well and incubate at RT with gentle shaking for 5 minutes
Add 500 µl/well Wb and shaking incubation for 5 min and remove buffer (Repeat this step minimum 3 times)
Remove DAPI
You have your slices ready for the microscope
Put the cover slide in a dark box and leave it to dry
Mount the slices on the slides. Cover with approximately 50 µl of VECTASHIELD® HardSet™ per slice
creator: Aroa S Maroto
